title: Go 1.26 and amqp091-go 1.12 Client Contract · Language Lens: Notification Channel Ownership
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must use amqp091-go APIs, errors, notifications, contexts, and version pins without hiding protocol meaning; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the notification channel ownership increment in a reviewed Go client API and lifecycle contract. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Register and continuously drain close, return, confirm, blocked, cancellation, and state-change notifications without blocking dispatch or leaking goroutines.
Treat Delivery fields, properties, headers, and body bytes as untrusted data and validate decoded Go values before domain use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Notification Channel Ownership · Theory Notification Channel Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 4594 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate delivered bytes and amqp091 notification ownership without mistaking Go types for validation; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then make one state notification stale after recovery generation changes. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Register and continuously drain close, return, confirm, blocked, cancellation, and state-change notifications without blocking dispatch or leaking goroutines.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Notification Channel Ownership contract for the supplied data.
